en.extract:
The Somali fiscal (Lanius somalicus) is a species of bird in the family Laniidae. Other common names include the Karoli fiscal, the Somali fiscal shrike and the Somali shrike. The bird is found in Djibouti, Ethiopia and Somalia in the Horn of Africa, as well as in Kenya in the African Great Lakes region. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical dry shrubland.


Distribution and habitat
The Somali fiscal Is found in East Africa, its range including much of Somalia, southern Djibouti, eastern and southern Ethiopia, southeastern South Sudan and northern Kenya. It is mainly found in open grassy habitats and semi-desert areas with some scrub. Where its range overlaps that of the Taita fiscal (Lanius dorsalis), that bird usually inhabits more densely-vegetated and less arid habitats.


Ecology
Like other shrikes, the Somali fiscal perches in a position where it has a good view all round and pounces on any suitable prey it observes. It feeds mostly on large insects such as grasshoppers, mantises and beetles. Prey is sometimes impaled on thorns for consumption later, and small birds have sometimes been found in these larders. During the breeding season, it will defend the area around its nest, being prepared to attack any bird that ventures near up to the size of a raven. Breeding usually takes place in the rainy season. The nest is usually in a bush, some 1 to 1.5 m (3 to 5 ft) above the ground, with a clutch of four, or sometimes three, eggs being laid. Little is known of incubation and fledging times.


Status
The Somali fiscal has a wide range and is reported to be common in places. The population trend appears to be steady so the International Union for Conservation of Nature has listed the species as being of "least concern".


References

[[File:Somali Fiscal P1019921 DxO.jpg|thumb|Somali Fiscal]]
The '''Somali fiscal''' ('''''Lanius somalicus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Laniidae]]. Other common names include the '''Karoli fiscal''', the '''Somali fiscal shrike''' and the '''Somali shrike'''.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?lang=EN&avibaseid=F79321856B22FEE2 |title=Somali Fiscal (''Lanius somalicus'') Hartlaub, 1859 |author=Lepage, Dennis |work=Avibase |access-date=2014-05-30}}</ref> The bird is found in [[Djibouti]], [[Ethiopia]] and [[Somalia]] in the [[Horn of Africa]], as well as in [[Kenya]] in the [[African Great Lakes]] region. Its natural [[habitat]] is [[subtropical or tropical dry shrubland]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

==Distribution and habitat==
The Somali fiscal Is found in East Africa, its range including much of Somalia, southern Djibouti, eastern and southern Ethiopia, southeastern South Sudan and northern Kenya. It is mainly found in open grassy habitats and semi-desert areas with some scrub. Where its range overlaps that of the [[Taita fiscal]] (''Lanius dorsalis''), that bird usually inhabits more densely-vegetated and less arid habitats.<ref name=Lefranc/>

==Ecology==
Like other shrikes, the Somali fiscal perches in a position where it has a good view all round and pounces on any suitable [[Predation|prey]] it observes. It feeds mostly on large insects such as grasshoppers, mantises and beetles. Prey is sometimes impaled on thorns for consumption later, and small birds have sometimes been found in these larders. During the breeding season, it will defend the area around its nest, being prepared to attack any bird that ventures near up to the size of a [[raven]]. Breeding usually takes place in the rainy season. The nest is usually in a bush, some {{convert|1|to|1.5|m|ft|0|abbr=on}} above the ground, with a clutch of four, or sometimes three, eggs being laid. Little is known of incubation and fledging times.<ref name=Lefranc>{{cite book|author1=Norbert Lefranc |author2=Tim Worfolk |title=Shrikes|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=HclLAQAAQBAJ&pg=PA152 |year=2013 |publisher=A&C Black |isbn=978-1-4081-8756-2 |page=152}}</ref>

==Status==
The Somali fiscal has a wide range and is reported to be common in places. The population trend appears to be steady so the [[International Union for Conservation of Nature]] has listed the species as being of "[[Least-concern species|least concern]]".<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

zh.extract:
索马里伯劳（学名：Lanius somalicus）是伯劳科伯劳属的一种。分布于吉布提、埃塞俄比亚、肯尼亚和索马里。其自然栖息地为亚热带或热带的干燥疏灌丛。


参考文献
